title: Real Estate Deals in Virginia Beach VA: The Investor's Complete Guide for 2024
excerpt: Virginia Beach is one of Virginia's most dynamic real estate markets, offering strong rental demand, solid appreciation, and hidden wholesale opportunities. This guide breaks down the best neighborhoods, deal types, and strategies for finding profitable real estate deals in Virginia Beach VA.
author: AIWholesail Team
tags: real estate deals in Virginia Beach VA
metaDescription: Find profitable real estate deals in Virginia Beach VA. Explore top neighborhoods, market trends, wholesale and rental strategies, and tips for out-of-state investors.
metaKeywords: real estate deals in Virginia Beach VA, Virginia Beach real estate investing, wholesale deals Virginia Beach, Virginia Beach rental properties, flip houses Virginia Beach, off-market deals Virginia Beach, Virginia Beach investment property

sections.content: Virginia Beach is not just a tourist destination with beautiful Atlantic coastline. It is also one of the most resilient real estate markets on the East Coast. Whether you are a seasoned wholesaler, a first-time flipper, or a long-distance landlord, finding profitable real estate deals in Virginia Beach VA is very achievable if you know where to look. This guide gives you the full picture — from current home prices and neighborhood breakdowns to deal types and out-of-state investor tips. If you are just getting started, we also recommend checking out our [Real Estate Investing for Beginners](https://aiwholesail.com/guides/real-estate-investing-for-beginners) guide before diving in.
Virginia Beach Market Overview: Why Investors Are Paying Attention

Virginia Beach is the most populous city in Virginia with roughly 460,000 residents. The local economy is anchored by a massive military presence — Naval Station Norfolk is nearby, Joint Expeditionary Base Little Creek is inside city limits, and Oceana Naval Air Station sits right in the heart of the market. This military base concentration creates something most real estate markets dream about: a steady, rotating pool of renters who receive housing allowances and need quality homes every 2 to 3 years. That single factor makes Virginia Beach one of the most landlord-friendly markets in the Southeast.

Beyond the military, Virginia Beach benefits from a thriving tourism economy, a growing tech and defense contracting sector, and proximity to the Port of Virginia — one of the fastest-growing ports in the country. Unemployment has historically tracked below the national average, and population growth has remained steady even as some coastal markets softened. The combination of job stability, population demand, and limited buildable land near the coast gives Virginia Beach real estate a solid long-term foundation.
Average Home Prices in Virginia Beach

As of mid-2024, the median home price in Virginia Beach sits around $340,000 to $360,000, up roughly 5 to 7 percent from the prior year. That puts it well below markets like Northern Virginia, Washington DC, and Richmond's premium zip codes, making entry points more accessible for investors. Single-family homes in working-class neighborhoods can still be found in the $200,000 to $280,000 range, which is the sweet spot for wholesale and fix-and-flip deals. Oceanfront and resort area properties climb dramatically — sometimes exceeding $1 million — so investors focused on returns rather than appreciation should stay away from the beachfront corridors. Use our free [ARV Calculator](https://aiwholesail.com/tools/arv-calculator) to quickly estimate after-repair value before making any offer on a Virginia Beach property.
Best Neighborhoods for Real Estate Investors in Virginia Beach
Bayside and Pembroke

The Bayside and Pembroke areas sit in the northern and central parts of the city and offer some of the best value for investors. Home prices here range from $220,000 to $320,000, and rental demand is strong due to proximity to military facilities and major employment hubs. Many homes are 1970s and 1980s ranches and split-levels that need cosmetic updates — ideal for wholesalers and light flippers. Gross rental yields in these neighborhoods regularly hit 7 to 9 percent, which is strong by Virginia standards.
Chesapeake Beach and Princess Anne

These neighborhoods in the southern corridor of Virginia Beach attract long-term family renters and owner-occupants. Properties here are slightly newer, which means fewer hidden repair surprises for flippers. The area is growing fast due to new commercial development along the Virginia Beach Boulevard and Indian River Road corridors. Investors who get in now before infrastructure improvements are complete tend to see the best appreciation gains.
Oceanfront and Resort Strip

The oceanfront area is best suited for short-term rental investors running Airbnb or VRBO strategies. Properties here carry higher price tags and lower cap rates on a traditional rental basis, but strong seasonal tourism can push gross annual revenue well above what long-term rents would generate. Know the local short-term rental ordinances before purchasing — Virginia Beach has regulated STR activity in certain zones.
Types of Real Estate Deals Available in Virginia Beach

items: Wholesale Deals: Aging housing stock in Bayside, Kempsville, and Pembroke creates consistent wholesale opportunities. Motivated sellers include retiring military families, estate sales, and absentee owners who have let properties fall behind. Assignment fees of $10,000 to $25,000 are realistic in this market.

Fix-and-Flip: Virginia Beach supports solid flip margins. A home purchased at $220,000, renovated for $40,000, and sold at $315,000 represents a net profit around $35,000 to $45,000 after holding and selling costs. Properties near military bases and good school zones move fast when priced right.

Long-Term Rentals (Buy and Hold): Military BAH (Basic Allowance for Housing) rates in the Hampton Roads area are generous. A 3-bedroom home renting for $1,800 to $2,200 per month is common in mid-tier neighborhoods, and military tenants often take excellent care of properties.

Short-Term Rentals: The oceanfront and surrounding resort areas support strong Airbnb performance from May through September. Investors with the right property type and management setup can generate $35,000 to $60,000 annually in gross rental income.

BRRRR Strategy: Buy, rehab, rent, refinance, repeat works well in Virginia Beach given the combination of available distressed inventory and strong rental demand. The key is buying below 70 percent of ARV to leave room for the refinance to recover your capital.

sections.content: Pro Tip: Military move cycles peak in the summer months (May through August). If you are a landlord or wholesaler in Virginia Beach, time your listings and marketing to hit the market between April and July to capture peak demand from incoming service members looking for housing fast.
Local Market Trends to Watch in 2024 and Beyond

Virginia Beach is experiencing a few key trends that smart investors are tracking closely. First, inventory remains tight. Active listings are still running 20 to 30 percent below pre-pandemic norms, which keeps competition high for retail buyers and creates urgency among motivated sellers who want a fast, quiet transaction — exactly what wholesalers offer. Second, the city is investing heavily in its Town Center district and the Virginia Beach Resort area, driving commercial activity and increasing desirability for nearby residential neighborhoods. Third, flood zone mapping updates are creating anxiety among some homeowners in low-lying areas, which is generating motivated seller leads for investors who understand how to navigate elevation certificates and flood insurance costs. To find [off-market real estate deals](https://aiwholesail.com/guides/off-market-real-estate-deals) in these pockets, direct mail and driving for dollars remain highly effective local strategies.

"The best real estate markets are not the flashiest ones. They are the ones with stable employment, population demand, and a reliable pool of renters who need housing year after year. Virginia Beach checks all three boxes." — Common wisdom among Hampton Roads buy-and-hold investors
How to Analyze Deals in Virginia Beach Correctly

Whether you are wholesaling or flipping, running accurate numbers is non-negotiable. Virginia Beach has active MLS data, which makes pulling comps relatively straightforward — but you need to be precise about neighborhood boundaries. A house in a flood zone two blocks from an identical property in a non-flood zone can have dramatically different ARVs. Always verify flood zone status using FEMA's flood map tool and factor in flood insurance costs when underwriting any deal near the coast or tidal waterways. For a step-by-step walkthrough of how to evaluate any deal properly, read our guide on [how to analyze real estate deals like a pro](https://aiwholesail.com/guides/how-to-analyze-real-estate-deals). You can also run your numbers instantly using our free [Wholesale Deal Calculator](https://aiwholesail.com/tools/wholesale-deal-calculator) to check your maximum allowable offer before submitting.
Tips for Out-of-State Investors Targeting Virginia Beach

items: Build a local team first: You need a boots-on-the-ground property manager, a reliable contractor, and a title company familiar with Virginia wholesale transactions before you close your first deal.
Understand Virginia contract law: Virginia uses a slightly different purchase agreement structure than some states. Work with a real estate attorney who handles investor transactions regularly.
Target military-adjacent zip codes: Zip codes 23455, 23464, and 23452 consistently produce the strongest rental demand and the most predictable tenant base for out-of-state landlords.

Use property management data: Ask local PMs what their average days-on-market for rentals look like and what the eviction timeline is in Virginia Beach. Virginia's eviction process is relatively landlord-friendly compared to many coastal states.

Visit the market at least once: Even one 3-day trip to walk target neighborhoods, meet contractors, and sit down with a local agent or wholesaler will dramatically improve your deal-finding ability and help you avoid overpaying.

Leverage technology: Platforms like AIWholesail let you search distressed property data and motivated seller signals for the Virginia Beach market without needing to be on the ground full-time. Explore the [Virginia Beach market dashboard](https://aiwholesail.com/markets/virginia-beach-va) to start identifying deal opportunities.

sections.content: Pro Tip: If you are new to wholesaling and want to practice your deal analysis before committing real capital to Virginia Beach, read our [Wholesaling Real Estate for Beginners](https://aiwholesail.com/guides/wholesaling-real-estate-for-beginners) guide. It covers how to find sellers, calculate your max offer, and assign contracts without using your own money.
Is Virginia Beach Right for Your Investment Strategy?

Virginia Beach is not a get-rich-quick market, and that is actually a good thing. It is a stable, demand-driven market with genuine fundamentals behind it. Wholesalers will find consistent motivated seller leads, especially from aging homeowners in older neighborhoods and absentee military landlords who are ready to exit. Flippers will find solid margins if they buy right and manage renovation costs tightly. Buy-and-hold landlords will find one of the most reliable tenant pools on the East Coast. The key to success here — as in any market — is knowing your numbers, building the right local relationships, and moving quickly when good deals surface.
